steps.observed: Identical top-2 results. Position 3 differs in noise results (score < 0.004). Semantically equivalent.

evidence.network: Embedding API calls for fresh index (1301 embeddings)

frictions.description: First vera update after initial index creation showed 224 files modified (all files) rather than detecting only the single modified file. This appears to happen when the index was built at a different time and file timestamps have changed. After running update twice to stabilize, subsequent updates correctly detect only changed files.
frictions.resolution: Ran vera update twice to establish baseline before testing single-file modification. Second run correctly shows 0 changes, confirming the index tracks filesystem state after first full sync.

frictions.description: Minor search result ranking differences between incremental and fresh index for low-relevance results (scores < 0.01). Top relevant results are consistent. This is expected behavior with stochastic embedding APIs.
frictions.resolution: Accepted as expected — embedding model produces slightly different vectors on re-encoding. Core behavior (same files, same stats) is consistent.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. VAL-INCR-001: Single file modification correctly triggers re-indexing of only that file (1 modified, 224 unchanged). New content is searchable, existing content unaffected. VAL-INCR-002: File add correctly detected (+1 file, +3 chunks), content searchable. File delete correctly detected (-1 file, -3 chunks), content removed from index. VAL-INCR-003: After full add/modify/delete sequence, incremental index matches fresh index exactly (225 files, 1301 chunks, identical language breakdown). Search results consistent with minor ranking noise in low-relevance results.
